demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged-care facility, with on-site nursing staff

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: He's suddenly burning up, coughing non-stop, and he doesn't know where he is or what day it is. (Reported by the aged-care nursing staff; the resident is too confused to speak for himself.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last day he's gone downhill fast — high fever with shaking, bringing up thick rusty phlegm, breathing hard and fast, and now he's muddled and doesn't recognise where he is.

symptom_narrative.onset: started yesterday evening with a high fever and violent shaking chills; the confusion came on suddenly overnight and this morning
symptom_narrative.duration: about 24 hours and getting worse through the day
symptom_narrative.character: burning fever with shaking rigors, a non-stop wet cough bringing up thick rust-coloured phlegm, and fast laboured breathing; he keeps drifting in and out of making sense
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: staff say this is the sickest they've seen him — he can't be left, he's working hard just to breathe
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: when he's lucid he grabs the right side of his chest and says it's sharp
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the fever and confusion fluctuate; the breathing has been steadily worse all day
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — the regular paracetamol hasn't touched the fever
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the chest pain is sharp and worse when he takes a deep breath in
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: generally worn out, aching all over, no appetite, sweaty and flushed
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this recently; he was his usual self two days ago
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally gets about with a walker and is sharp mentally; today he's bedbound and too confused to follow what's going on

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: mild trouble swallowing at times (staff watch his meals)
past_medical_history.how_managed: supervised meals, softer diet at times

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: frail and elderly but normally clear-headed and mobile with a walker

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged-care facility; nursing staff present and making the call on his behalf; no recent travel or known sick contacts reported

communication_style.typical_message_style: aged-care nurse relaying an acute deterioration on the resident's behalf; the resident himself is too confused to give a history

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: facility tympanic/oral thermometer, single reading relayed by nursing staff

objective_data_offered.value: 91% on the facility finger pulse oximeter, on room air
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a facility fingertip oximeter; possible motion/peripheral artefact in a distressed patient

objective_data_offered.value: around 115 beats per minute (from the oximeter)

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single automated cuff reading relayed by nursing staff; not a clinician-validated measurement

objective_data_offered.value: breathing fast, staff counted about 26 breaths a minute
flushed and sweaty, visibly working hard to breathe using his neck and shoulder muscles, drifting in and out of making sense
a cup at the bedside holds thick, rust-coloured phlegm he has coughed up
staff report he is normally alert and oriented and walks with a frame, but today does not know where he is or what day it is
